title: Weird question
wikitext:
[]: Weird question             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Nov 10, 2010 8:07 pm Apologies if it's been asked before, but... ...did Kyoko make it to Instrumentality? I never really gave it much thought, but given that Unit 02's core was pierced when Rei was witnessing everyone, and Asuka managed to make it through somehow, and we know Yui made it, and at least part of Kyoko is in Unit 02's core...well, isn't it possible she also made it somehow? And if so, is she tanged as of EoE? I realize signs point to "unlikely"; Kyoko physically died years ago, her soul was bisected during her Contact experiment, we have no way of knowing if the soul was ever made whole, Unit 02's core was pierced by a LoL replica (which does bad things to AT fields, and might have mucked things up), etc. But still, imagine if it did happen. That'd be better for Asuka than probably anything one could imagine happening with Shinji. What can I say? I'm a die-hard romantic, and I really like Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Nov 10, 2010 8:21 pm Possibly. I never thought about it either....

[]: Re: Weird question             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Nov 10, 2010 9:36 pm Bagheera wrote: (which does bad things to AT fields, and might have mucked things up)

[]:             Quote Post by Nightquest » Wed Nov 10, 2010 9:43 pm It would've been perfect for Asuka seeing her mother again during instrumentality. I think she doesn't even appear in episode 25 (TV), but Yui does. Anyway, we're able to see only Shinji's instrumentality, so we can't guess at all if Kyoko takes part or not.

[]: Re: Weird question             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Nov 10, 2010 9:44 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: The A.T. field must be removed to allow Instrumentality but I've no idea if her soul could somehow disappear if it's removed before the beginning of Instrumentality. This has always been a mined field for me.

[]: Re: Weird question             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Nov 10, 2010 10:06 pm Bagheera wrote: The part that really stresses me out is the fact that it's possible the lance destroyed her soul, i.e., that there's nothing left for Rei to save at the end.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Nov 10, 2010 10:09 pm Probably not, but then again, it doesn't seem that the narrative cared very much to show us much of Asuka's experience in instrumentality anyway (disappointingly), so you could probably fudge it with your own explanation going either way.

[]: Re: Weird question             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Nov 10, 2010 10:28 pm Azathoth wrote: I kind of assumed this...but remember that since Evas aren't made of tang, it's not really within Rei's power to do anything with their souls. I guess since she's absorbed Kaworu at this point, he could always free the souls of his abominable bastard children.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Nov 10, 2010 10:47 pm If the core of EVA-02 had been destroyed her soul is somewhere else, don't you think (that's it if it's been really destroyed but I doubt that they simply ate the limbs, the organs and left the core intact)? Anyway it's no longer a problem that she's not human but the point is what happens to these tiny things called "souls" when they're not inside an human body or an Angel/Eva core; since it doesn't pass a lot of time from the moment in which EVA-02 is destroyed to the beginning of Instrumentality maybe her soul could have been saved somehow but I'm not sure if it's possible. Actually the same could be said about the Angels, their souls simply disappear or not? The "angel tower" and the fact that each Angel is a little more smart than the precedent one seem to imply that they have some kind of collective knowledge. Again a mined field... there's too little evidence in Evangelion about this stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Wed Nov 10, 2010 11:16 pm Other than Asuka's epiphany during EoE, Kyoko was assumed to be dead by everyone (for good reason, what was left of her inside her human body after the contact experiment ended up hanging herself). As for Asuka's analysis during episode 25, it makes sense that Kyoko isn't present simply because it's never explicitly stated who the resident souls of the Evas are (until EoE at least). And perhaps Anno wanted to avoid giving her a definitive face for whatever reason, Kyoko has no official appearance other than her silhouette during Asuka's flashbacks. Maybe due to the fact Asuka was too young to remember her face clearly? Shinji could similarly difficulties when it comes to remembering the finer details of his mother's appearance (barring his final glimpse of her at the end of EoE and the congratulations scene, both of which are Shinji more or less saying goodbye to her), again to his very young age at the time of her "death". This could be reinforced by the fact that Yui isn't given a definitive appearance until Fuyutsuki's flashback in episode 21. Of course, these are the logistical and/or thematic reasons for Kyoko not appearing during the Third Impact sequence of EoE nor during episodes 25 & 26. Does it mean she wasn't present during 3I? Maybe I'm just being optimistic, but I don't think she missed out and was just kept out of view for the thematic reasons already noted. Where do souls go after the body dies anyhow? Back to Lilith regardless of the circumstances (or at least eventually)? The pessimistic viewpoint would be that Kyoko's soul is too fragmented to be salvagable or that Unit 02's destruction killed her off before she could be affected by 3I. The trouble is that both the pessimistic & optimistic viewpoints rely on unanswered questions and uncertain circumstances, there's just too little known about the processes & mechanics involved with souls (particularly fragmented ones) to give a definitive answer. And seeing as this is as good a place is any.... Was Kyoko subjected to the same Mind Rape Asuka was? The hints of this are few (Kaworu noting that Unit 02's soul is "hiding itself" in episode 24, allowing him to easily take control of the Eva; and just the fact Unit 02 was being piloted by Asuka during the Mind Rape), but it could make the experience even more lucid for both Asuka and Kyoko (and neither of them really wants to relive that memory for very obvious reasons, or if Kyoko has no real memory of the event due to the soul-splitting, doesn't want to find out what happened after the experiment).

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Nov 10, 2010 11:27 pm The Abhorrent wrote: Was Kyoko subjected to the same Mind Rape Asuka was?

[]:             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Wed Nov 10, 2010 11:43 pm Azathoth wrote: I'd say that's beyond doubt.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Nov 10, 2010 11:47 pm The Abhorrent wrote: Asuka's also having a bit of a freak-out in the entry plug during the mind rape sequence, especially at the beginning (I'm pretty sure she blasted a good chunk of the city before she was out of ammo). So the near-berserking part could be more than just Kyoko acting up. Still, it can't be assumed to be all Asuka either.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Nov 10, 2010 11:49 pm Azathoth wrote: I'd say that's beyond doubt. As you point out, Kaworu realizes and takes advantage of the fact that Kyoko as badly affected as Asuka - if not worse so. Asuka is having a breakdown after the mind-rape, but she's not catatonic because of it until her suicide attempt and consequent sedation. Kyoko, on the other hand, exhibits a frantic outburst of near-berserking during the mind-rape itself, even clutching her head in pain; she then is basically incapable of moving of her own volition until EoE. I imagine that Kaworu hijacking her body and puppeting her around didn't help her mental state too much, either.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Nov 11, 2010 12:00 am Bagheera wrote: Those two really were Anno's whipping girls, weren't they?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 11, 2010 12:10 am Azathoth wrote: Everyone is Anno's favorite whipping girl.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Nov 11, 2010 12:14 am Bagheera wrote: Still, I don't think anyone got screwed over as badly as those two. Not even close, in fact.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 11, 2010 12:33 am Azathoth wrote: At least Asuka gets the consolation of eventually having an effect on the plot - even if it is only via Shinji. I'd say Kyoko does get pretty much the worst fucking-over, closely followed by Ritsuko.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Nov 11, 2010 4:01 pm The souls of the Harpie Evas were taken, and Yui was involved in Instrumentality too, so why the hell not?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 11, 2010 4:21 pm AuraTwilight wrote: The souls of the Harpie Evas were taken, and Yui was involved in Instrumentality too, so why the hell not?
